content.problemStatement:
There are NNN stones numbered 111 through NNN. Each stone has a value written on it; for each valid iii, the value on stone iii is ViV_iVi‚Äč.

You are initially on the first stone and your goal is to jump to stone NNN by perfoming a valid sequence of jumps. Formally, let's denote a sequence of visited stones during these jumps by S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SMS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_MS1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SM‚Äč, where M‚ąí1M-1M‚ąí1 is the number of jumps. Then, the following conditions must be satisfied:


1=S1<S2<‚Ä¶<SM=N1 = S_1 \lt S_2 \lt \ldots \lt S_M = N1=S1‚Äč<S2‚Äč<‚Ä¶<SM‚Äč=N
for each iii (1‚Č§i<M1 \le i \lt M1‚Č§i<M), min‚Ā°(VS1,VS2,‚Ä¶,VSi)+max‚Ā°(VSi+1,‚Ä¶,VSM)=Si+1‚ąíSi\min(V_{S_1}, V_{S_2}, \ldots, V_{S_i}) + \max(V_{S_{i+1}}, \ldots, V_{S_M}) = S_{i+1} - S_imin(VS1‚Äč‚Äč,VS2‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,VSi‚Äč‚Äč)+max(VSi+1‚Äč‚Äč,‚Ä¶,VSM‚Äč‚Äč)=Si+1‚Äč‚ąíSi‚Äč, which is the length of the iii-th jump
Find the minimum number of jumps needed to reach stone NNN, or report that it is impossible to reach stone NNN using valid jumps.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers V1,V2,‚Ä¶,VNV_1, V_2, \ldots, V_NV1‚Äč,V2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,VN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer --- the minimum number of jumps, or ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if it is impossible to reach stone NNN.
constraints: 1‚Č§Vi‚Č§N1 \leq V_i \leq N1‚Č§Vi‚Äč‚Č§N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500500500
**Subtask #1 (15 points):** Vi‚Č§2V_i \le 2Vi‚Äč‚Č§2 for each valid iii
**Subtask #2 (35 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 100100100

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: You can jump from stone 111 to stone NNN directly because min‚Ā°(V1)+max‚Ā°(VN)=2\min(V_1) + \max(V_N) = 2min(V1‚Äč)+max(VN‚Äč)=2 and the length of the jump is exactly 222.
Example case 2: It is impossible to reach stone NNN.
Example case 3: One valid sequence of visited stones which uses the minimum number or jumps is (1,5,9,11)(1, 5, 9, 11)(1,5,9,11). It is valid because the lengths of the jumps are:

first jump: min‚Ā°(V1)+max‚Ā°(V5,V9,V11)=4\min(V_1) + \max(V_5, V_9, V_{11}) = 4min(V1‚Äč)+max(V5‚Äč,V9‚Äč,V11‚Äč)=4
second jump: min‚Ā°(V1,V5)+max‚Ā°(V9,V11)=4\min(V_1, V_5) + \max(V_9, V_{11}) = 4min(V1‚Äč,V5‚Äč)+max(V9‚Äč,V11‚Äč)=4
third jump: min‚Ā°(V1,V5,V9)+max‚Ā°(V11)=2\min(V_1, V_5, V_9) + \max(V_{11}) = 2min(V1‚Äč,V5‚Äč,V9‚Äč)+max(V11‚Äč)=2
